## Summary

Adds interactive analytics charts and insight cards to the user-facing `/dashboard` page, wired to real evaluation data. Users now see score trajectories, error type trends, and craft improvement insights — charts and graphs to keep heavy users excited and motivated.

## Scope

**What this touches:**
- `app/dashboard/page.tsx` — imports analytics data layer + renders analytics section below existing AuthorProgressLedger
- `lib/dashboard/getDashboardAnalytics.ts` — new data layer querying `evaluation_artifacts` (criterion scores) and `diagnostic_findings` (error counts) for all completed evaluations
- `components/dashboard/charts/ScoreTrendChart.tsx` — Recharts interactive line chart: Overall + Market Readiness scores over time, dashed 8.0 market-ready threshold, hover tooltips with manuscript title/date/exact score
- `components/dashboard/charts/ErrorTrendChart.tsx` — Recharts grouped bar chart: diagnostic finding counts by criterion across evaluations, top 8 categories, color-coded
- `components/dashboard/charts/InsightCards.tsx` — Most Improved, Still Blocking Readiness, Recent Wins cards computed from real data
- `components/dashboard/charts/DashboardAnalyticsSection.tsx` — wraps all chart components with manuscript selector filter (All Manuscripts / individual manuscript toggle)

**What it does NOT touch:**
- AuthorProgressLedger existing UI
- Evaluation pipeline, exports, admin pages

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Recharts is already in package.json (`^2.15.4`) and has existing UI components in `src/components/ui/chart.jsx` — no new dependency
- Chart components use `dynamic()` with `ssr: false` since Recharts requires client-side rendering
- For users with no completed evaluations, analytics section gracefully hides (returns null)
- Insight cards show placeholder text when insufficient data exists for trend computation

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — user dashboard UI only, no evaluation pipeline changes
